title: horkelia californica subsp. dissita
intro: Horkelia californica subsp. dissita, also known as California horkelia or dissita horkelia, is a perennial wildflower native to California, characterized by its white or pink flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it useful in treating various health issues such as sore throats, coughs, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Horkelia californica subsp. dissita include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, pneumonia, and other respiratory infections, as well as its ability to support the immune system and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Horkelia californica subsp. dissita can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
